account_targeting_signal.rationale: Segmentation is razor-sharp — 5000+ employee German energy utility with SAP IS-U, KRITIS obligation, NIS2 registration, and smart meter rollout. My AEs know exactly which 20-25 Großversorger this fits. Market parameters (EUR 1B+ revenue, 5000+ employees, energy vertical) map directly to named account lists we already maintain.

pain_to_pitch_mapping.rationale: Every pain point maps to a portfolio proposition. SAP IS-U migration (2027 deadline) → cloud-migration. OT/IT convergence (SCADA/IEC 61850) → hybrid-cloud. Fachkräftemangel cloud ops → managed-hyperscaler. NIS2/KRITIS-DachG compliance → sovereign-cloud. Zero orphan pain points. AE reads a pain point and immediately knows which proposition deck to pull.

committee_navigation.rationale: Four distinct profiles with a clear multi-threading play. CIO is the champion (bring the business case), CISO is independent veto (lead with BSI-C5 and NIS2), Einkauf enforces SektVO (bring the Rahmenvertrag structure), Netzleiter validates OT (bring the air-gap architecture). Champion sequence in buying_committee gives my AE the exact call order. Pain points are completely distinct per role — no overlapping conversations.

objection_readiness.rationale: Strong objection map — BSI vetting 3-6 months, SektVO tendering 2-4 months, Betriebsrat 1-3 months, Haushaltszyklus planning window (June-September miss = 12 month delay). The Haushaltszyklus stall point is excellent sales intelligence — most competitor profiles miss this. Minor gap: no explicit competitive displacement guidance. When the AE faces an incumbent (Atos, Accenture, SAP direct), what's the specific switching cost argument? The buying criteria imply it (Konzerneinbindung = DT advantage) but it's not spelled out.

named_customer_quality.rationale: Named customers not yet generated — this is expected since named customer research is a downstream step after profile validation. The ICP-level intelligence is strong enough that my AE team can start prioritizing accounts from our existing territory lists immediately.

sales_cycle_realism.rationale: 5-8M EUR ACV, 9-18 month cycle, 6 timed stall points — these numbers match my pipeline data for energy sector cloud deals. The stall point timing adds up correctly: 3-6mo BSI + 2-4mo SektVO + 1-2mo Vorstand = 6-12 months of non-selling time inside an 18-month cycle. This is exactly what I see in my forecast reviews.

top_issues.issue: No competitive displacement guidance — when my AE faces an incumbent SI (Atos, Accenture, SAP direct), the profiles don't tell them what the switching cost argument looks like or how to position T-Systems' DT/sovereign advantage against specific competitors
top_issues.suggested_fix: Add a 'competitive_displacement' note to the buying_committee object covering the top 3 incumbent scenarios (SAP direct, Atos/Accenture outsourcing, hyperscaler-direct) and T-Systems' counter-positioning for each
top_issues.root_cause_hint: customer skill scope — competitive intelligence may be out of scope for ICP profiles

strengths: Zero orphan pain points — every buyer concern maps to a portfolio proposition, so my AE never uncovers a problem they can't solve
Haushaltszyklus planning window stall point (June-September) is sales intelligence gold — most profiles miss this timing-critical blocker
Buying committee champion_sequence is a deal playbook — CIO initiates, Netzleiter validates, CISO vets, Einkauf tenders, Vorstand approves — my AE knows the exact meeting sequence
Distinct pain points per role enable true multi-threading — the CIO conversation is fundamentally different from the CISO conversation
overall_assessment: These profiles are immediately actionable for my sales team. An AE can read this Monday morning and start building account plans by Monday afternoon — they know who to call (CIO first), what to lead with (SAP IS-U migration deadline pressure), what objections to expect (BSI vetting, SektVO tender, Haushaltszyklus timing), and how to multi-thread across four distinct committee roles. The only gap I'd close before enablement is competitive displacement guidance — how to position against Atos/Accenture/SAP direct when they're already incumbent. But that's a nice-to-have, not a blocker. I would deploy these profiles to my energy sector team this week.
